creator: mp@FreeBSD.org
text:
I compiled a recent 12.0-current (r322037) from last night and then compiled open-vm-tools-nox11 from ports. Both vmblock.ko and vmxnet.ko load without a panic for me. However, I do see a panic when vmmemctl.ko is loaded with this panic:

VMware memory control driver initialized
suspending ithread with the following locks held:
exclusive rw vm object (vm object) r = 0 (0xfffff8001f1d0c30) locked @ os.c:522
panic: witness_warn
cpuid = 0
time = 1501868737
KDB: stack backtrace:
db_trace_self_wrapper() at db_trace_self_wrapper+0x2b/frame 0xfffffe011b6e0880
vpanic() at vpanic+0x19c/frame 0xfffffe011b6e0900
kassert_panic() at kassert_panic+0x126/frame 0xfffffe011b6e0970
witness_warn() at witness_warn+0x415/frame 0xfffffe011b6e0a20
ithread_loop() at ithread_loop+0x175/frame 0xfffffe011b6e0a70
fork_exit() at fork_exit+0x84/frame 0xfffffe011b6e0ab0
fork_trampoline() at fork_trampoline+0xe/frame 0xfffffe011b6e0ab0
--- trap 0, rip = 0, rsp = 0, rbp = 0 ---
KDB: enter: panic

The os.c:522 is inside open-vm-tools os_kmem_alloc():
...
#if __FreeBSD_version > 1000029
      VM_OBJECT_WUNLOCK(state->vmobject); <====== line 522
#else
      VM_OBJECT_UNLOCK(state->vmobject);
#endif


Can you confirm whether you had compiled open-vm-tools after updating your kernel? Also, does the above panic look the same as the one you are seeing?
